examples.title: System V Message Queue Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates creating, sending, receiving, and removing a System V message queue
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates System V message queue operations: ftok() generates a unique key, msgget() creates the queue with read-write permissions, msgsnd() sends a message with type 1, msgrcv() receives messages of type 1, and msgctl() with IPC_RMID removes the queue.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of messages

examples.title: POSIX Message Queue - Producer
examples.description: Producer process creating and sending messages using POSIX API
examples.explanation: This producer code creates a POSIX message queue with mq_open() specifying max messages and size. mq_send() adds messages with priorities (4th parameter). Messages appear in /dev/mqueue filesystem. The queue persists until explicitly removed.

examples.title: POSIX Message Queue - Consumer
examples.description: Consumer process receiving messages from POSIX queue
examples.explanation: Consumer opens queue in O_RDONLY mode. mq_receive() retrieves highest priority message first. mq_getattr() displays queue properties. The loop continues until queue is empty (mq_receive returns -1 with EAGAIN or blocks waiting).

examples.title: Circular Queue Implementation
examples.description: Array-based circular queue implementation with enqueue and dequeue operations
examples.explanation: Circular queue uses modular arithmetic (index % MAX_SIZE) to wrap around. Key formulas: enqueue at (rear+1)%size, dequeue at (front+1)%size. This allows reusing freed positions efficiently. The size counter tracks number of elements.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for both enqueue and dequeue

examples.title: Priority Queue Implementation
examples.description: Simple priority queue using array with insertion sort for ordering
examples.explanation: Priority queue uses max-heap property where highest priority element is always at root. Insert adds at end then bubbles up. ExtractMax removes root, moves last element to root, then bubbles down. Time complexity: O(log n) for insert and extract.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) for insert and extract
